description_long: This stone object has a roughly T-shaped form with three prominent lobes extending outward from a central, vertically oriented mass. The surface is irregular and textured, with visible undulations and a natural, unpolished appearance, suggesting it is a naturally occurring or minimally worked stone. The top section is more cylindrical and rises above the base, while the lower extensions are broader and flatter, giving the object a stable footprint. The stone appears solid and heavy, with no visible cavities or openings, and its coloration is a uniform pale white, possibly indicating limestone or a similar sedimentary rock. The edges are rounded rather than sharp, and the overall shape is asymmetrical, with each lobe differing slightly in size and curvature. This type of stone could serve as a barrier or obstacle in urban environments, such as a park, sidewalk, or roadside, where it might be used to prevent vehicle access or delineate pedestrian zones.
description: A large, irregularly T-shaped white stone with a rough, natural surface and three broad lobes extending from a central mass. Its solid, heavy appearance and asymmetrical form make it suitable as an urban barrier or obstacle.
description_view_0: The stone is seen from above, highlighting its T-shaped form and the three broad lobes radiating from the central mass.
description_view_1: This side view emphasizes the vertical height of the central section and the elongated, flatter extension at the base.
description_view_2: The rear view shows the broad, rounded back and the full width of the lower extensions, with the central mass rising above.
description_view_3: Another side view, similar to view 1, but from the opposite side, showing the asymmetry of the lobes and the rough surface texture.
attribute_placement: set as a standalone feature or barrier on the ground
attribute_pattern: Irregular, natural mottling and undulations with no artificial carvings or regular patterns.
attribute_weathering: Rough and unpolished, with minor surface pitting and signs of natural weathering.
attribute_integration_with_surroundings: Contrasts with paved surfaces but may blend with landscaped areas or other natural stones.
